elements.title: John Mackenzie Moore
elements.contents: ... that 1,500 citizens petitioned Canadian architect John Mackenzie Moore (pictured) to run for election as mayor of London, Ontario, and he ultimately defeated the popular incumbent?

elements.title: Zebulon Devil Dogz
elements.contents: ... that the Zebulon Devil Dogz, a collegiate baseball team in the United States, is to consist entirely of athletes from Australia?

elements.title: The Assembly
elements.contents: ... that The Assembly premiered during Autism Acceptance Week?

elements.title: Gaynor Legall
elements.contents: ... that an audit led by Gaynor Legall found that Wales had more than 200 commemorations of people linked to the slave trade?

elements.title: Rock Me All Night Long
elements.contents: ... that the title phrase of the 1952 R&B song "Rock Me All Night Long" is a euphemism for sexual intercourse?

elements.title: Henry Morford
elements.contents: ... that Henry Morford wrote sequels to works by Charles Dickens and Lord Byron?

elements.title: a 1998 documentary
elements.contents: ... that a 1998 documentary covers economic exploitation in post-communist gay pornography from Eastern Europe?

elements.title: a museum exhibit
elements.contents: ... that a museum exhibit later became a Rockefeller family residence?

elements.title: a comic about how drugs are dangerous
elements.contents: ... that, after the US government asked Marvel Comics to make a comic about how drugs are dangerous, the Comics Code Authority refused to approve it because it was about drugs?
